1
MODULE 7
SUMMARY NOTES
Your Instructor:
Tom Breeze
Advertising on YouTube involves 4 elements:
1. Your customer’s moment
2. The perfect video ad
3. Reaching your customer in their moment
4. Walkthrough
4 Reasons We Go to YouTube
• I Want to Watch What I’m Into: “I’m interested in this stuff” aka window
shoppers
• I Want to Know Moments: “I’m researching and reviewing” aka in-store
shoppers
• I Want to Do Moments: “I’m researching and reviewing” aka in-store
shoppers
• I Want to Buy Moments: “I’m ready to buy” aka checkout shopper
The Perfect video ad structure makes conversion as easy as possible and
is specifically created around the customer moment:
• For window shoppers, your goal is to catch their attention through a
schema violation
• For in-store shoppers, your goal is to give them advice through
“aducation”
• For checkout shoppers, your goal is to get them to take an action
through a CTA
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

2
Module 7 Summary Notes
The perfect video ad structure uses 3 elements to create conversions:
• Schema violation
• Aducation
• CTA
Here’s a roadmap for what elements to include based off of what shopper
you’re targeting:
There are various targeting options and each of these options is the most
successful with specific shoppers:
• Window Shopper: similar audiences, affinity audiences, channel
placements
• In-Store Shopper: keywords, topics, video placements
• Checkout Shopper: in-market audience, remarketing
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 5 --

3
Module 7 Summary Notes
Other targeting options include:
• Age
• Gender
• Location
• Device
• Time of Day
• Household Income
• Parental Status
• Website Remarketing
• Video Remarketing
There are 2 different Video Ad types, in-stream and discovery.
In-Stream Video Ads: You only pay when a user watches more than
30-seconds of your ad or clicks to your website
Discovery Ads: You pay when a user clicks the thumbnail of your ad and
plays it
YouTube Ads work through Google Ads (formerly AdWords), so you’ll need
a Google Ads account to start advertising on YouTube
Upload your video ad to YouTube and keep it open in a tab while you’re
adding it to your Google Adwords account
To add your ad to your Google Adwords account, follow these
instructions:
1. 	In your Google Adwords account, go to All Campaigns and then click
the red button that says “Campaigns”, then click the “Video” option
from the drop-down menu
2. 	Name your campaign based off of the type of campaign and who it
is targeted for—For example “IS KEY [Desktop] US Males” is an in-
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 5 --

4
Module 7 Summary Notes
stream keyword campaign running on desktop to males living in USA
3. 	Choose the video ad funnel (in the above campaign name example,
you’d choose in-stream)
4. 	Choose “Cost Per View” for the bidding option
5. 	Choose a daily budget you are comfortable with (starting with at least
$10 so Google can acquire data)
6. 	Choose “Standard” Delivery method to spend your budget evenly
throughout the day
7. 	Under Networks, turn off “YouTube Search” and “Video Partners on
the Display Network” so only “YouTube Videos“ is checked
8. 	Choose your location of choice
9. 	Choose your language of choice
10. Choose your device adjustments based off of what devices you want
users to see your ad on
11. In advanced settings, you can create start dates and end dates for
ads that need to run in the future (for example, a product launch) and
schedule ads at certain points during the day specific to that day
12. For Ad delivery, you can choose to turn off ads after a specific
number of impressions and views per day per user to avoid users
seeing too many of your ads
13. Click Save and Continue to save your campaign
Inside of your campaign, you have Ad Groups where you can choose
what campaign to run:
1. 	Name your ad with the chosen keywords for that ad
2. 	Paste the URL of your video ad
3. 	Choose a Display URL
4. 	Optional: Upload your own image as a Companion Banner
5. 	Choose an Ad Name
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 5 --

5
Module 7 Summary Notes
6. 	Choose your Maximum Cost Per View (CPV), starting around $0.07
for a large audience
7. 	Leave Popular Video Bid empty
8. 	Choose your Target Audience based off of gender, age, parental
status and household income
9. 	Choose Interests based off of your target audience
10. Add the Keyword
11. Click Save Ad Group
Google will review the video and then make it live
Continue to optimize your keywords and CPC for the largest ROI
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com
